qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to run Materials Studio after it has been installed for a team?
qa_pairs.answer: After Materials Studio has been installed for a team, you must first execute the command `module use $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018/modules/2017/Core/`. Team members may opt to add this command to their `~/.bashrc` file. Following this, you can load the Materials Studio module using `module load materialsstudio`. Remember to substitute `PI_PROJECT_DIR` with its appropriate value.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for installing versions of Materials Studio older than 2018?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need to install a version of Materials Studio older than 2018, you must use an Apptainer container. This process involves creating an Apptainer container with a compatible Linux distribution, installing Materials Studio within that container, and then uploading and utilizing the container in your account. Additionally, you will need to contact technical support to configure firewalls, enabling the software to connect to your license server from compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What potential limitations might arise when running older versions of Materials Studio installed within an Apptainer container?
qa_pairs.answer: When running older versions of Materials Studio from an Apptainer container, you might be restricted to whole-node (single-node) jobs. This limitation can occur because the version of MPI included in the container may not support usage across multiple nodes.
